column5: Chapter Title (Mandatory)
column7: Question ID (Mandatory and Unique, for example: 1.1, 1.2, Etc)
column10: Answer Type (Mandatory, one of the following options: 1 for Open Answer, 2 for Dropdown - Single Select, 3 for Dropdown - Single Select - Open answer, 4 for Date, 5 for Dropdown - Multiple Select, 6 for Dropdown - Multiple Select - Open answer)
column11: Dropdown Options (Mandatory if column G is Dropdown. List options for the dropdown separated by a pipe character, for example: Yes|No|Undefined)
column12: DropDown Warnings (Optional, for every option on column H you can define a message, for example: Great|Oh|Think twice)
column13: Dropdown Scoring (Mandatory, set this to 1 if you want to enable scoring for this question. Otherwise set to 0)
column14: Dropdown Option Score (Mandatory if J is set to 1, set the score of the question as an integer positive number, for example 10)
column15: Dropdown Option Score Multiplier (Mandatory if J is set to 1. For every option of the dropdown in column H define an integer positive number that will be multiplied by the value on column K, for example: 1|2|10)
column16: Dropdown Option Show another Question (Optional and only possible if column G is dropdown.For every option in column H you can define one or more question IDs from column D to be shown when the option is selected. For example: 1.2 1.6||6.7 would display questions 1.2 and 1.6 if the first option is selected, none if the second is selected and 6.7 if the third option is selected)
column17: Question Comments & Attachments (Mandatory, select 0 if you do not want to show this option or 1 if you want to let users provide comments and attachments for each question)

column3: - Management: Ensures business continuity objectives are defined, resourced, approved, and reviewed. - The Information Security function: Defines information security requirements for business continuity, with the support of other department managers. - Business and Process Owners: Identify critical activities, support business impact analysis, and ensure continuity requirements are met. - IT / Operations Teams: Support the implementation, testing, and execution of continuity and recovery actions. - Suppliers and Service Providers: Support continuity requirements as defined in agreements. ---

column3: - Information security requirements must be integrated into business continuity planning. - Business continuity plans must: - Maintain existing information security controls during disruption where possible. - Define compensating controls when normal controls cannot be maintained. - Security of information must be restored to the required level within defined timeframes following disruption. - Continuity plans must be developed, implemented, tested, reviewed, and updated.

column3: - A Business Impact Analysis must be performed to: - Identify critical business activities. - Assess impact types and severity over time. - Determine acceptable disruption periods. - Each prioritized activity must be assigned a Recovery Time Objective (RTO). - Resources required to support prioritized activities must be identified. - For information supporting critical activities, a Recovery Point Objective (RPO) must be defined where applicable.

column2: Standard - Redundancy of Information Processing Facilities
column3: - Redundancy must be implemented where required to meet availability objectives. - Redundant components and facilities must provide equivalent security to primary systems. - Redundancy may include, where applicable: - Multiple network or connectivity providers. - Redundant networks or routing paths. - Geographically separated hosting locations. - Redundant power supplies or sources. - Multiple parallel software instances with load balancing. - Procedures must exist to activate redundant components. - Redundant systems must be monitored for failures. - Redundant systems should be tested to verify failover operates as intended. ---
column5: Does this requirement reflect your company current practice: Redundancy must be implemented where required to meet availability objectives.

column3: - Redundancy must be implemented where required to meet availability objectives. - Redundant components and facilities must provide equivalent security to primary systems. - Redundancy may include, where applicable: - Multiple network or connectivity providers. - Redundant networks or routing paths. - Geographically separated hosting locations. - Redundant power supplies or sources. - Multiple parallel software instances with load balancing. - Procedures must exist to activate redundant components. - Redundant systems must be monitored for failures. - Redundant systems should be tested to verify failover operates as intended. ---
column5: What evidence can you provide to demonstrate this requirement is implemented? Examples may include screenshots, reports, logs, tickets, approvals, procedures, meeting records, or system exports.

column2: Procedure - Continuity Review and Improvement Procedure
column3: 1. Review continuity plans at planned intervals. 2. Review plans after significant disruptions or incidents. 3. Update plans, controls, and documentation. 4. Report results to management. ---
